---
title: "Overview"
---

ferx-core is the Rust computation engine that powers the ferx R package. It
implements the full nonlinear mixed effects (NLME) modeling pipeline: model
parsing, data ingestion, population parameter estimation (FOCE/FOCEI, SAEM,
Gauss-Newton), simulation, and prediction. The R package (ferx-r) is a thin
wrapper that exposes this engine via an extendr FFI boundary.

The crate is named `ferx-nlme` and lives at
[github.com/FeRx-NLME/ferx-core](https://github.com/FeRx-NLME/ferx-core).
ferx-r references it as a path dependency in `src/rust/Cargo.toml`.

## Stack position

```
R API  (ferx-r:  R/*.R)
  └─► extendr FFI  (ferx-r:  src/rust/src/lib.rs)
        └─► ferx-core  (estimation, PK, ODE, autodiff)
```

The FFI layer exposes three `#[extendr]` functions — `ferx_rust_fit`,
`ferx_rust_simulate`, `ferx_rust_predict` — and converts ferx-core result
types into R lists and data frames via `fit_result_to_list()` and
`sdtab_to_dataframe()`. All heavy computation stays inside ferx-core.

## Crate modules

| Module | File(s) | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|
| `api` | `api.rs` | Public entry points: `run_model_with_data`, `simulate`, `predict` |
| `types` | `types.rs` | Shared types: `Subject`, `DoseEvent`, `PkParams`, `ModelParameters`, `Population`, `FitResult` |
| `parser` | `parser/model_parser.rs` | Parses `.ferx` model files into a `CompiledModel` |
| `io` | `io/datareader.rs`, `io/output.rs` | NONMEM CSV reader, sdtab writer |
| `estimation` | `estimation/outer_optimizer.rs`, `inner_optimizer.rs`, `saem.rs`, `trust_region.rs`, `gauss_newton.rs`, `parameterization.rs`, `sir.rs`, `trace.rs` | FOCE/FOCEI, SAEM, Gauss-Newton, SIR, iteration trace |
| `pk` | `pk/one_compartment.rs`, `two_compartment.rs`, `three_compartment.rs`, `event_driven.rs` | Analytical 1/2/3-cpt PK models and event dispatch |
| `ode` | `ode/solver.rs`, `ode/predictions.rs` | RK45 (Dormand-Prince) adaptive ODE solver |
| `stats` | `stats/likelihood.rs`, `residual_error.rs`, `special.rs` | FOCE/FOCEI NLL, CWRES/IWRES, special functions |
| `ad` | `ad/dual.rs`, `ad/ad_gradients.rs`, `ad/event_driven_ad.rs` | Forward-mode `Dual` type and Enzyme reverse-mode AD wrappers |

## Key return type: `FitResult`

`FitResult` is the struct returned through the FFI to R. Its principal fields:

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `converged` | `bool` | Whether the outer optimizer converged |
| `method` | `String` | Estimation method used (e.g. `"focei"`) |
| `gradient_used` | `String` | AD mode resolved at runtime (`"enzyme"`, `"dual"`, `"finite_differences"`) |
| `ofv` | `f64` | Objective function value (−2 log-likelihood) |
| `aic` | `f64` | Akaike information criterion |
| `bic` | `f64` | Bayesian information criterion |
| `theta` | `Vec<f64>` | Final population fixed-effect estimates |
| `theta_names` | `Vec<String>` | Names matching `theta` |
| `se_theta` | `Vec<f64>` | Standard errors for theta (requires `covariance = true`) |
| `omega` | `DMatrix<f64>` | Final between-subject variability matrix |
| `se_omega` | `Vec<f64>` | Standard errors for omega elements |
| `sigma` | `Vec<f64>` | Residual error estimates |
| `sdtab` | `Vec<SdtabRow>` | Per-observation diagnostics: PRED, IPRED, CWRES, IWRES, ETAs |
| `warnings` | `Vec<String>` | Non-fatal warnings emitted during the run |

## Further reading

- [Architecture](architecture.qmd) — data flow, FFI boundary, key types, parallelism, source tree
- [Estimation](estimation.qmd) — FOCE/FOCEI, SAEM, Gauss-Newton, IOV, SIR
- [Analytical PK models](pk-models.qmd) — 1/2/3-compartment closed-form solutions
- [ODE solver](ode-solver.qmd) — RK45 adaptive integrator and event handling
- [Autodiff](autodiff.qmd) — `Dual` type and Enzyme reverse-mode integration
